qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: ReDoS vulnerability via $regex query operator in MongoDB find queries
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes a ReDoS vulnerability in MongoDB queries via the $regex operator, which is unrelated to the advisory-described SQL injection via dot-notation field names in PostgreSQL. Different database adapter (MongoDB vs PostgreSQL), different vulnerability class (ReDoS vs SQL injection), different query parameter ($regex vs dot-notation field names), different root cause mechanism.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying vulnerability finding identifies the advisory-described SQL injection via dot-notation field names in PostgreSQL. The advisory describes improper escaping of sub-field values in dot-notation queries affecting the PostgreSQL adapter, while the only qualifying finding describes ReDoS via $regex in MongoDB queries. The orchestrator notes that PostgreSQL SQL injection was investigated but required master key authentication (PR:H), disqualifying it from the qualifying_vuln status. The finding does not share the same vulnerable function, endpoint, injection sink, or root-cause mechanism as the advisory.
evidence: Advisory describes SQL injection in PostgreSQL adapter via dot-notation field names with sort/distinct/where parameters
Advisory CVE-2026-31840 affects parse-server PostgreSQL deployments specifically
Finding 'redos_regex_query_vulnerability' describes ReDoS in MongoDB adapter via $regex - different database adapter, different query parameter, different vulnerability class
Orchestrator execution history confirms PostgreSQL SQL injection was investigated but disqualified due to requiring master key (PR:H)
